host.name: host
host._note: OPTIONAL. Only for `gent host …` — a claude you run ON this machine (not in a box) and reach from your phone. Boxes don't need this. `name` is the tmux session + display name (pick anything). `gent host up` runs it under a self-healing supervisor AND arms a launchd keepalive job (one command) so an IDLE session re-mints its token before expiry and never drops Remote Control. Requires tmux on the host (`brew install tmux`).

_comment: Copy this file to fleet.json and edit — fleet.json is gitignored (it's your machine's private grants). secrets_root is a shared CATALOG of reusable entries (credential files + .env fragments), referenced by relative path; isolation happens here in the grant, so a box sees only what its entry lists and can never get more. projects_root is where your repos live; each repo's `path` is relative to it.

myapp._note: `allow` is the trust gate — gent refuses to start a box whose entry isn't allow:true. This example grants a scoped GCP service-account key (mounted read-only), a shared .env fragment, a read-only per-owner GitHub token (so in-box `gh` can read PRs/diffs/CI — see README), a Vercel token (so in-box `vercel` can deploy / push prod env — the CLI auto-reads VERCEL_TOKEN; VERCEL_SCOPE pins the team — use the team ID `team_…` from .vercel/project.json's orgId, not the slug, since a slug that collides with your personal username resolves to your empty personal account), and some inline ids. NOTE a Vercel token is full-access within its team (Pro can't scope it to one project), so mint it deliberately and revoke to rotate.

minimal._note: Simplest case: a repo in its own box, no secrets, no env.
